protocol.name: deck_gamepad_v1
protocol.copyright: Copyright (C) 2026 Braiins Systems s.r.o. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

interface.name: deck_gamepad_manager_v1
interface.description:
Creates a gamepad input object for a wl_surface owned by the client.
The compositor owns device discovery, hotplug, stable player slots, and focus routing. The protocol deliberately carries Linux event codes and raw axis values: controller-specific layouts and application semantics remain client policy rather than compositor policy.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Existing gamepad input objects remain valid.

request.name: get_gamepad_input

description.summary: subscribe a surface to focused gamepad input

request.description: Creates one input object associated with surface. Connection metadata is reported regardless of focus. Button, axis, and frame events are delivered only while surface is the compositor's gamepad focus.

interface.name: deck_gamepad_input_v1

description.summary: raw reports from gamepads routed to one surface

interface.description:
Player slots remain stable while a physical controller is connected and are reclaimed by the same physical path after a reconnect when possible.
Axis metadata follows connected and precedes axis values for that player. A frame event commits all preceding button and axis changes from one EV_SYN report. Clients should update semantic state at frame boundaries.

event.name: connected

description.summary: a controller occupies a stable player slot

event.description: name is diagnostic only. Clients should select a mapping from vendor, product, and the raw event capabilities that follow.

event.name: axis_info

description.summary: range for one Linux EV_ABS code

event.description: minimum is strictly lower than maximum. The compositor sends this metadata after connected and before values for the same axis.

description.summary: the player slot no longer has a controller

event.name: button
event.description: code is a Linux input-event key code. Repeats are folded into pressed.

event.name: axis
event.description: value is in the inclusive range advertised by axis_info.

event.name: frame
event.description: Applies all button and axis events for player since its previous frame.
